description: Fast-food or QSR brands announcing limited-time weekend promotions with specific pricing, product names, and drive-through/pickup convenience messaging to drive immediate customer action.
structural_recipe: Lead with 'عرض الويكند' (Weekend Offer) announcement using megaphone emoji. Feature specific item name with clear pricing in SAR using 'بـ X ريال' format. Include combo/bundle messaging ('مع أي وجبة'). Add convenience indicator (🚗💨 for drive-through, delivery capability). Use exclamation marks and action-oriented language. Keep copy short and scannable. Pair with product close-up or lifestyle shot.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers plan weekend activities mid-week and respond strongly to time-bound deals. Drive-through culture is dominant in Riyadh/Jeddah. Explicit pricing removes friction and builds trust. Arabic-first approach signals accessibility and local authenticity. Emojis break up text and increase visual scannability on mobile feeds.
transplantation_caveats: Pricing psychology differs by region—weekend vs. weekday deals may not drive equal engagement outside Saudi Arabia
Drive-through infrastructure reference only relevant in countries with similar QSR convenience culture
Deal timing must align with actual weekend (Friday-Saturday in Saudi context, not Saturday-Sunday)
VAT and pricing transparency expectations are high in Saudi market—avoid ambiguous or hidden fees
cultural_constraints: Friday prayer timings may affect deal redemption window—avoid 11am-2pm messaging
Family-group consumption is primary use case; messaging should reflect group value, not individual
Avoid alcohol references or non-Halal product positioning
